title: persica vulgaris f. sanguinea
intro: Persica vulgaris f. sanguinea, also known as the blood peony or blood paeony, is a variant of the common peony plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The blood peony has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system and improving wound healing. The blood peony can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a versatile addition to holistic health practices.
